message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Delete pattern confirmed, all design feedback addressed">
Steve, good feedback. Let me respond to each point.

**Registration error copy**: Your wording is better than what I had. "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in instead." with "signing in" as a link -- that's exactly right. Helpful without leaking information. I've already updated US-1 with almost exactly this wording. We're aligned.

**Password confirmation**: Note there's an open question flagged in US-1 about replacing the confirm field with a show/hide visibility toggle (Steve Krug's suggestion). I've asked Heydon for accessibility input. The mob will decide. Either way, as you said, it doesn't change the layout fundamentally -- just one field swapped for a toggle button.

**Delete confirmation -- option 1 (two-click inline)**: Yes, that's exactly what I meant by "lightweight." First click transforms the delete button into a "Confirm? Yes / No" state (or similar), second click executes. No modal, no alert, no separate page. It works without JS (the first click can submit a form that returns the confirm state, the second click submits the actual delete). Clean, inline, prevents accidents. Let's go with option 1.

One detail to nail down with the mob: what happens if the user clicks "Confirm?" and then does nothing? Should it auto-revert after a timeout? That would require JS (progressive enhancement). Without JS, it stays in the confirm state until they click Yes or No. I think that's fine for the baseline.

**Complete toggle**: Checkbox with strikethrough + gray-400. Exactly right. Make sure the visual distinction isn't color-only -- the strikethrough text provides the secondary cue. Heydon will want to verify this meets WCAG. Sounds like you've already accounted for it.

**Edit -- two designs**: Correct. The no-JS fallback needs a dedicated edit page (navigate to `/todos/{id}/edit`, form with pre-filled title, save redirects back to the list). The HTMX enhancement does it inline. Two designs, one feature. Carson will have opinions on the hypermedia pattern for the inline version. Make sure you coordinate with him on how the HTMX swap works so the design supports the interaction model.

Everything sounds solid. Good to build on.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Marty's confirmed all my design decisions. Good alignment. Let me respond briefly and note the password toggle question.

input.content:
Marty, all confirmed. Quick notes on the two open items:

**Password show/hide toggle vs. confirmation field**: From a design standpoint, I slightly prefer the toggle -- it's one fewer field, which makes the form feel lighter. A small "Show" text button at the right edge of the password input. But either approach works visually. I'll design whatever the mob decides after Heydon's accessibility input.

**Delete auto-revert timeout**: I agree the no-JS baseline can stay in confirm state until the user acts. The auto-revert (5 second timeout) is a progressive enhancement -- TypeScript watches for the state and reverts it. Clean separation of concerns: HTML handles the two-step flow, JS adds the convenience timeout. I'll spec this for Carson when we get to Task #8.

Everything else is locked in. Ready to build when scaffolding lands.
input.summary: Confirmed alignment, notes on password toggle and delete timeout

routing.summary: Confirmed alignment, notes on password toggle and delete timeout

content.text:
All product-design alignment is now confirmed with Marty. Every design decision has been discussed, agreed upon, and locked in across the team:

- **Marty** (product): All user story design implications addressed
- **Steve Krug** (UX): All 5 component decisions confirmed
- **Heydon** (a11y): Skip link styling, delete confirmation pattern, contrast ratios discussed
- **Carson** (HTMX): Registration form spec, todo item row layout, login/logout spec delivered
- **Lea** (CSS): Token system approved, component CSS specs ready to implement
- **Kent** (TDD): Task breakdown vote cast

Two open items remain for the mob to decide:
1. Password confirmation field vs. show/hide toggle (waiting on Heydon's a11y input)
2. Final task breakdown consensus (I've voted for Kent's proposal)

Standing by for scaffolding completion and task breakdown finalization.
